journals.title: The Final Ledger
journals.body:
It’s done. The dead-man’s switch is coded, the crypto wallet is funded, and the evidence package is set to deliver itself to the SEC in 48 hours unless I manually input the stand-down code. It feels less like a victory and more like signing my own death warrant. Every time Julian touches my arm, I flinch. He looks at me and sees a signature, a legal entity to absorb the impact of their fraud. He doesn’t see a person anymore, if he ever did.

Sloane keeps asking about the vows. 'Have you finished them? Let me see!' she texts, with a dozen heart emojis. I told her I want them to be a complete surprise, even to her. It’s a necessary lie. She’s my oldest friend, but she’s also so enmeshed in their world now, so dependent on their generosity. I can’t risk her loyalty being tested. The vows are the final key. Not a promise to him, but a promise to myself. Every line, every word, is a step away from this gilded cage. This has to work. There is no plan B. The boat is ready, the new identity is waiting. I just have to walk out of this hotel room and not look back.

journals.title: Paranoia or Instinct?
journals.body:
I can't shake the feeling I'm being watched. Not by Julian's official security—I know their faces, their rotations. This is different. A car that's been parked across the street for two days. A 'maintenance worker' I've never seen before near the service elevator. Am I just letting the pressure get to me? Marcus warned me that in an operation like this, the weakest link is always human psychology. You start seeing ghosts in every shadow.

But what if they know? Not the whole plan, maybe, but enough. What if Julian’s father, that cold-blooded patriarch, suspected I was too smart to be a willing scapegoat? He's the one I'm truly afraid of. Julian is a puppet, a spoiled child playing with a corporate bazooka. His father is the one who loads it and aims it. I went over the financials again last night. The shell companies, the wire transfers... it's so blatant, so arrogant. They believe they're untouchable. They've built an empire on the assumption that everyone else is either too stupid to notice or too scared to act. They're about to find out they misjudged me on both counts.

journals.title: For Leo
journals.body:
Saw Leo's 'One Year Sober' post on his private Instagram. A whole year. I almost broke down in the middle of this ridiculous, gold-plated hotel suite. I remember the night he called me, the night he hit bottom. The thought of him, of his strength, is the only thing keeping me grounded right now. Everything I'm doing, the insane risk, the money... it's not just for my freedom. It's to build something for us. A safe place, far away from the Crofts and their poison.

I wired him some money a few weeks ago, told him it was an early wedding gift. It was the first clean cash I could pull out. Enough for him to have a cushion, no matter what happens to me. He thinks I'm living a fairytale. 'My sister, the millionaire bride!' he joked. It hurts to lie to him, to let him believe in this fantasy. But it's the only way to protect him. If Julian knew how much Leo means to me, he'd use him as leverage in a heartbeat. He's the only family I have left, the only person in this world who sees Harper Vance, not the future Mrs. Croft. I have to believe that once this is over, I can explain it all and he'll understand.

journals.title: The Sinking Ship
journals.body:
Julian cornered me this evening after the rehearsal dinner. He was drunk, reeking of expensive scotch and desperation. He was talking about the future, about 'our legacy,' but his eyes were glassy with fear. He knows something is wrong with the company's finances. He doesn’t know the details—his father keeps him on a need-to-know basis, which is almost nothing—but he can feel the ground shifting beneath his feet. He grabbed my wrist, a little too tight, and said, 'Everything is riding on us, Harper. On this merger of families.' He meant 'on you.'

It was the confirmation I needed. My plan isn't just an escape; it's a lifeboat. This whole dynasty is a house of cards, and the slightest breeze of legal scrutiny will bring it all down. For a second, I almost felt pity for him. He's just a scared little boy, terrified of disappointing a monstrous father. But then I remembered the condescending way he talks to waitstaff, the casual cruelty, the affair I know he's having. He is a product of his environment, and the entire ecosystem is toxic. I can't save him. I can only save myself. The pity is gone. Now, it's just resolve.

journals.title: My Vows to Freedom
journals.body:
I’ve finalized the vows. Reading them aloud feels like casting a spell. On the surface, they are words of love and commitment, filled with the flowery language everyone expects. But underneath, woven into the very fabric of the sentences, is my declaration of independence. The acrostic is perfect. Subtle, but undeniable once you know what you're looking for. It's the key to everything. The 24 words that will unlock my new life. It feels dangerous and exhilarating to hide my escape in plain sight, to make the instrument of my entrapment—the wedding ceremony itself—the trigger for my liberation.

Sloane is practically begging to read them. 'I have to make sure you don't sound like a robot!' she said. I laughed it off, but the refusal was firm. This is the one part of the wedding I've kept completely to myself. It feels like the only part that's truly mine. The dress, the venue, the guest list... all of it was chosen by the Croft machine. But these words? These words are my weapon. They are the last thing I will ever 'give' to Julian Croft, and he'll never even understand their true value. It's the perfect, silent revenge.

journals.title: If you're reading this...
journals.body:
If you're reading this, it means I didn't make it. It means they caught me, or Sloane did, or something went terribly wrong on the water. It means the dead-man's switch has triggered, and the world knows what the Crofts are, but I'm not there to see it. I'm not a fool. I knew the risks. I knew that stealing five million dollars and a lifetime of secrets from men like the Crofts was a dangerous game. But it was a better option than the alternative: a slow, legal death as their designated scapegoat.

Please, whoever you are, get this phone to my brother. Leo Vance. He'll know what to do. Tell him not to mourn me, but to live. To live freely and fully, away from all of this. That's all I ever wanted. Tell him the money is his. The keys are in my vows. Tell him the password is his name. He was always the key to my courage anyway. And please, make sure Julian and his father pay for what they've done. Don't let them spin this story. Don't let them turn me into a villain or a tragic victim. I was a fighter. I fought for my life and my freedom. I just hope, in the end, that it was enough.

journals.title: Things I'll Miss (Not Julian)
journals.body:
It's strange, the things you think about when you're about to erase your entire life. It’s not the grand stuff. Not the parties or the travel or the designer clothes. It’s the small, quiet moments that I know I’ll ache for. The smell of old books in a library. The specific way the light hits the lake at dawn. Leo’s stupid laugh when he tells a bad joke. The feeling of solving a complex problem, that little click in your brain when the solution appears. My worn-out copy of 'Meditations'. Rainy Sunday afternoons with a cup of tea and no obligations.

I’m trading it all for a future that is a complete unknown. It's terrifying. But then I think about the things I won't miss. Julian’s empty compliments. His father’s dissecting stare. The suffocating pressure to be 'perfect.' The constant, low-grade hum of being owned, of being a beautiful, well-behaved asset. The way Sloane looks at me sometimes, with a flicker of something that isn't friendship—envy, maybe, or resentment. When I weigh the two lists, the choice is clear. I will mourn the small joys, but I will not miss the gilded cage. Freedom is worth more than all of it.

journals.title: The Vows (Final Draft)
journals.body: Julian, from the moment we met, I knew my life was about to change. Always, you have shown me a world of unbelievable heights. Never did I imagine a love so grand. Every day with you feels like a dream. Very few people are lucky enough to find their perfect match. Only you could make me feel this way. Underneath it all, I know we have a bond that is unbreakable. Really, I do. Never have I been more sure of anything. So today, I stand here ready to begin. Another chapter starts now. Near or far, I will always be yours. Do you feel the same? All my love, all my life. You are my everything. Now and forever. Keep this promise with me. Every moment led to this. You are my destiny.
journals.image_prompt: A close-up of a document written in an elegant script font on high-quality stationery. The title is 'My Vows'. The text is visible but slightly blurred, suggesting it is a private and important document.

chats.text: Don't touch anything.
This device is under my protection. You are an unauthorized user.
Who is this? What's going on?
I'm the guy who can turn this phone into a very expensive paperweight. Who I am is not your concern. Who YOU are is my only concern. Identify yourself.
I'm the night manager at the hotel. I found the phone. The owner, Harper Vance, is missing. It's her wedding day.
I know who she is. I know what day it is. 'Found it' is a convenient story. Prove it. What's the name of the hotel?
The Grand Hotel Tremezzo. I'm not trying to cause trouble, I just want to help find her.

Okay. You check out. For now. Listen carefully. Harper is in trouble. The people she's involved with, the Crofts, are not your average rich family. They are dangerous. Do not, under any circumstances, call the police or hand this phone over to hotel security. They own both.
What do you want me to do?

I need access to the phone's file system, but I'm being firewalled. I'm going to push a file to the home screen. It's an encrypted spreadsheet. I need you to find a way to open it. It's the key to everything. Don't disappoint me.
Harp, you seeing this? One year. ONE WHOLE YEAR. Couldn't have done it without you.
chats.image_prompt: A screenshot of an Instagram post. It shows a simple '1 Year' chip, the kind given for sobriety milestones, held in a person's palm. The caption is heartfelt and thankful.

chats.text: That's amazing Leo! I am so, so proud of you. You did the work. You deserve all the credit.
Nah. You were my one phone call. That matters. Anyway, you ready for the big day? Don't trip walking down the aisle lol
Haha, I'll try my best. Just nervous. A lot going on.
You got this. That guy doesn't deserve you, you know. But as long as you're happy, I'm happy. See you tomorrow.
J, we have a problem.
Sloane, I told you not to text me today. Too risky.
She knows. I don't know how, but she knows about us.
Don't be paranoid. She's just stressed about the wedding. How could she know?
The way she looks at me. It's different. Cold. We need to be careful.
Just get through the next 48 hours. Act normal. After that, it won't matter.
It already matters. What about the boat?
What about it?
We need to talk about the boat. Tonight.
They're in. Croft's IT. Trying to wipe the device. I'm fighting them but they're brute-forcing it.
What can I do?
I'm creating a partitioned safe zone. Anything you can drag into the 'SECURE' folder on the desktop, I can protect. Prioritize documents, photos, anything that looks like evidence. GO.
They're targeting the gallery. I'm losing packets. Some files are going to be corrupted. Better than nothing. Keep working.
Okay. I've stabilized it. They're locked out... for now. They know we're here. This just got a lot more complicated. What did you save?

emails.subject: FINAL ITINERARY: Croft-Vance Wedding Day!
emails.body:
Dearest Harper and Julian,

The big day is finally here! We are all so thrilled and honored to be a part of your story. The weather forecast is perfect, the flowers have arrived, and everything is in place for a truly magical day.

Please find the final timeline attached. A quick summary for the bridal party: Hair and Makeup begins at 9:00 AM sharp in the Bellagio Suite. Photos on the terrace at 1:00 PM. All members of the wedding party must be at the Lakeside Villa by 3:30 PM for procession lineup. The ceremony begins promptly at 4:00 PM.

Julian, your car will be waiting at the main entrance at 3:00 PM. Harper, we have champagne and light snacks ready for you and the bridesmaids. Try to relax, breathe, and soak in every single moment! It's going to be a day to remember forever. Sending all our love and best wishes.

emails.subject: SOW - Final Invoice
emails.body:
Harper,

Attached is the final invoice for services rendered under SOW-HV77B, 'Digital Redundancy Project.' All assets are in place. The primary dead-man switch is tied to the designated crypto wallet's primary transaction key. Any attempt to access the funds without the secondary biometric key will trigger the data packet release to the specified recipients. The 48-hour countdown is armed and will activate upon my signal or a system wellness check failure.

The secondary system, codenamed 'Cerberus,' is monitoring all network traffic to and from Croft-owned devices, including the one you're holding. I've built a backdoor with root access, but it's fragile. If they suspect anything and initiate a proper enterprise-level wipe, I may not be able to stop it completely. My ability to intervene will be limited.

Be smart. Be careful. Once you start, the only way out is through. Payment of the attached invoice is due upon your successful 'relocation.'

emails.subject: Urgent: Pre-Nuptial Addendum C
emails.body:
Ms. Vance,

Per our conversation yesterday, I am attaching the final version of the pre-nuptial Addendum C for your signature. As discussed, this addendum grants you a board seat and nominal voting shares in three of Croft Real Estate's subsidiary holdings: 'Apex Global Ventures,' 'North Star Holdings,' and 'Silver Compass LLC.'

This is a gesture of goodwill from the family, a symbol of your full integration into the Croft corporate structure. It is imperative that we receive your signed copy before the ceremony. The family considers this a non-negotiable final step in formalizing your union with Julian. Failure to sign will be interpreted as a material breach of the wedding agreement.

Please have your legal counsel review it at their earliest convenience, though I assure you it is standard procedure. We look forward to officially welcoming you to the family.

emails.subject: Transaction Confirmation: Test Transfer Successful
emails.body:
Dear User HV_Crypto_Strategist,

This is an automated notification to confirm that your recent test transaction has been successfully completed. 

Amount: 0.001 BTC
Recipient Wallet: 1A1zP1eP5QGefi2DMPTfTL5SLmv7DivfNa
Transaction ID: a1075db55d416d3ca4202f78bde0f0f67e5a9163e5d9852f1220b70232462804

Your account is active and fully functional for sending and receiving assets. For security, remember to store your 24-word seed phrase in a safe, offline location. CoinVault staff will never ask you for your seed phrase or password.

Thank you for choosing CoinVault.

emails.subject: Evidence Package - Croft Real Estate Fraud Division
emails.body:
To whom it may concern at the Securities and Exchange Commission,

This email and the attached encrypted data package have been sent via an automated system. If you are reading this, it means I, Harper Vance, am either missing, incapacitated, or deceased.

For the past eighteen months, I have served as the unofficial Chief Strategist for Croft Real Estate, engaged to Julian Croft. In that time, I have discovered and documented a massive, ongoing scheme of investor fraud, money laundering, and embezzlement orchestrated by CEO Alistair Croft and his son, Julian. The scheme involves a network of shell corporations (Apex Global Ventures, North Star Holdings, Silver Compass LLC, among others) used to inflate asset values and siphon funds from investor capital. I believe they were grooming me to be the legal scapegoat for when their scheme inevitably collapsed.

The attached package contains internal ledgers, incriminating emails, altered financial statements, and a detailed map of the money trail. I have done my best to annotate the evidence for clarity. I was planning to disappear to protect myself from the Croft family, whom I consider to be ruthless and dangerous. My disappearance should be considered a direct result of their criminal activities. Please use this information to see that justice is served.

emails.subject: Re: Missing Person Report - Harper Vance (Case #LC2024-4815)
emails.body:
Mr. Croft,

Thank you for your call. I am writing to confirm we have opened an official missing person investigation for your fiancée, Ms. Harper Vance, Case #LC2024-4815. We are treating this matter with the utmost seriousness and urgency.

As per our conversation, we have officers conducting a search of the hotel grounds and interviewing staff. We understand your concern regarding media attention and are doing our best to handle this situation with discretion. However, we must follow procedure. We will need to collect personal items from Ms. Vance's room for forensic analysis and will require a formal, in-person statement from you at your earliest convenience.

We have also been made aware of an incident involving Ms. Vance's brother, Mr. Leo Vance, at your family's villa this morning. He is currently in custody. We understand this is a difficult and complex family situation, but we must insist that you and your family cooperate fully with our investigation. Any information, no matter how small, could be crucial. We will be in touch shortly to schedule your interview.

receipts.description:
1x Industrial Cable Ties (100-pack)
1x Heavy-Duty Duct Tape
1x Box Latex Gloves
1x Bottle Antiseptic Solution

Ride requested by Sloane Davis.
Pickup: Grand Hotel Tremezzo (2:45 AM)
Dropoff: Como Marina (3:02 AM)
Car: Black Mercedes Sedan

CASH WITHDRAWAL
ACCOUNT: **** **** **** 882E (J. CROFT)
LOCATION: ATM-04, Cernobbio
AVAILABLE BALANCE: 1,243.56

DEPOSIT - 1 WEEK RENTAL
VESSEL: 28ft Riva Aquarama 'La Tempesta'
RENTER: H. Vance
PAID IN FULL. KEY PICKUP: FRI 6:00 AM

voice_notes.speaker: Harper Vance
voice_notes.transcript: Okay, uh, test one. So, if anyone asks... No, that sounds fake. Let's try again. 'I just needed some air. The pressure was... it was too much. I had to get away and think.' Is that believable? Maybe too cliché. What about... what about anger? 'I found out about the affair. I couldn't stand there and marry him. I just... I ran.' Yes, that's better. It's closer to the truth, anyway. It gives them a motive they can understand. A jilted bride. They'll never look for the real reason. They'll never look for the money.

voice_notes.speaker: Harper Vance
voice_notes.transcript: (Sound of rustling paper) Okay, let's practice the vows one more time. 'Always you have shown me... a world of unbelievable heights. Never did I imagine... a love so grand. Every day with you feels... like a dream.' It sounds so... saccharine. Ugh. But it has to. It has to sound like something I would write. Something the blushing bride would say. No one can suspect. 'Very few people... only you could... underneath it all...' Just got to get the rhythm right. The rhythm is the key. Focus, Harper. Focus on the words. They're going to set you free.

voice_notes.speaker: Leo Vance
voice_notes.transcript: (Panting, background noise of a struggle, muffled shouting) Harper, it's me! Listen, you need to get out of there! It's Sloane! She's been lying to you, to everyone! I saw... I saw texts on Julian's phone. About a boat, about... (Sound of a door slamming open) Hey! Get your hands off me! (To someone else) I'm on the phone with my sister! (Muffled) Julian, you coward! You won't get away with this! (Line goes dead)

voice_notes.speaker: Sloane Davis
voice_notes.transcript: (Voice is quiet, breathy, almost rehearsed) 'Oh my god, Julian? It's Sloane. I... I can't find her. Her room is empty. Her phone is going straight to voicemail. I'm so scared. What do we do? What if something's happened to her?' ... No, too calm. More panic. (Takes a deep breath) 'JULIAN! Oh my god, you need to get here right now! Harper's gone! I think... I think something terrible has happened!' ... Yes. That's better. That's the one.

social_posts.author: Julian Croft
social_posts.content: Waiting for my beautiful bride. The longest minutes of my life. Can't wait to see you, @HarperVance. #CroftVanceWedding #LakeComo #Love
social_posts.image_prompt: A handsome man in a tuxedo, looking wistfully out over Lake Como from a beautiful villa balcony. The photo is professionally taken, with a golden hour glow.

social_posts.author: Sloane Davis
social_posts.content: Has anyone seen my best friend? Harper, if you see this, please just call me. We're all so worried. Please, just let us know you're safe. #FindHarper #LakeComo
social_posts.image_prompt: A selfie of a teary-eyed young woman. Her makeup is slightly smudged. The background is a blurry hotel corridor. The image looks urgent and unpolished.

social_posts.author: Julian Croft
social_posts.content: This is a nightmare. We’ve contacted the authorities but I’m begging anyone with any information about my fiancée, Harper Vance, to please come forward. We are desperate. My heart is broken. Please pray for her safe return.

social_posts.author: Carol Vance
social_posts.content: Sharing this beautiful picture from Harper's college graduation. She has always been so brilliant and strong. My daughter is a fighter. Please keep her in your thoughts and prayers today. We are waiting for you, sweetie.
social_posts.image_prompt: A slightly dated photo of a proud mother hugging her daughter, who is wearing a graduation cap and gown. They are both smiling brightly.

social_posts.author: Como Chronicle
social_posts.content: BREAKING: Leo Vance, brother of missing bride Harper Vance, has been arrested following an altercation at the Croft family villa in Cernobbio. Sources say he assaulted fiancé Julian Croft. Developing story.

social_posts.author: Financial Crimes Today
social_posts.content: MASSIVE NEWS: SEC has filed federal fraud charges against Croft Real Estate CEO Alistair Croft and his son Julian Croft. Sources cite a major data leak from an internal whistleblower. Trading on $CROFT has been halted. #SEC #Fraud #Croft

social_posts.author: Sloane Davis
social_posts.content: Can't believe it's finally here! So excited to watch my bestie marry the man of her dreams! #weddingday #maidofhonor #besties #como
social_posts.image_prompt: A short, upbeat video of two young women laughing and clinking champagne glasses on a hotel balcony. They are in silk robes. Text overlay reads 'It's Wedding Day!'

social_posts.author: Sloane Davis
social_posts.content: My sister, my other half. From building forts in the backyard to this. There are no words to say how happy I am for you. #TBT #BestFriendsForever #CroftVanceWedding
social_posts.image_prompt: A faded, slightly grainy photo of two young girls, around 8 years old, with their arms around each other, smiling with missing teeth.

lines.speaker: Julian Croft
lines.text: Harper? Harper, pick up! Where are you? The ceremony is in less than four hours! Sloane said you weren't in your room. This isn't funny. If this is some kind of cold feet prank, it's cruel. My father is here, the guests are arriving... You're embarrassing me. You're embarrassing us. Just... just call me back. Now.

lines.speaker: Leo Vance
lines.text: (Sounding frantic) Harper? Oh, thank god. Who is this? Where's my sister?

lines.speaker: Protagonist
lines.text: I'm the hotel manager. I found her phone. We don't know where she is.

lines.speaker: Leo Vance
lines.text: Hotel... okay, listen to me. You can't trust anyone there. Especially not Julian. And not Sloane.

lines.speaker: Protagonist
lines.text: Sloane? Her maid of honor? She's been trying to organize a search party.

lines.speaker: Leo Vance
lines.text: It's an act! It's all an act! I know she's involved. I saw something, texts between her and Julian. Something about a boat. I'm heading to the villa now to confront him.

lines.speaker: Protagonist
lines.text: Is that a good idea? The police are involved now.

lines.speaker: Leo Vance
lines.text: The police work for the Crofts in this town! I have to do this myself. Just... keep the phone safe. Don't give it to anyone. It's the only... (Sound of a car door, shouting in the background) I have to go. Don't trust Sloane!

lines.speaker: Alistair Croft
lines.text: (Voice is cold, furious) Explain what? That you let your fiancée, the one person who could burn us to the ground, slip through your fingers? That the SEC is federalizing our assets as we speak because of a data packet she sent them?

lines.speaker: Julian Croft
lines.text: I didn't know! How could I know she was planning this?

lines.speaker: Alistair Croft
lines.text: Because it was your job to know! Your only job! To keep her happy, to keep her compliant! Instead you were playing games with the maid of honor. Did you think I didn't know about that?

lines.speaker: Julian Croft
lines.text: Sloane had nothing to do with this...

lines.speaker: Alistair Croft
lines.text: I don't care about your sordid little affairs! I care about my empire, which is currently crumbling because of your incompetence. Our lawyers are trying to get ahead of this, but the leak was catastrophic. You have made a mess, Julian. A mess that may be impossible to clean up. Find that phone.

messages.text: Has anyone heard from Harper? She's not picking up.
I just went to her suite, she's not there! I thought she was with you??
Maybe she went for a walk to clear her head? Big day jitters?
Her phone is going to voicemail now. I'm starting to get seriously worried.
Dude, chill. She'll turn up. She's probably at the spa or something.
No, Rick, this isn't like her. She's meticulously punctual. Her makeup was supposed to start 15 minutes ago. Something feels wrong. 😟
Oh my god. Okay, don't panic. Let's organize. Sloane and I will check the hotel grounds. Rick, you check the bar and restaurant?
Good idea. Everyone report back here in 30 minutes. Let's find her. 🙏
The SEC news is everywhere. CNN, Bloomberg... It's a total disaster.
Stay off social media. Do not talk to the press. Do not talk to anyone. Is that clear?
Alistair is correct. We are preparing a 'no comment' statement. The immediate priority is containment. We need to know exactly what was in that data packet she sent.
Everything. It must have been everything. The ledgers, the offshore accounts... she had access to it all.
This is your fault, Julian. I told you she was too clever to be a simple asset.

Finger-pointing is not productive. We need to focus on the phone. The authorities have it listed as missing property in evidence. But if it contains the crypto keys, it's the only leverage we have left. We need to get it back.
My security team is already working on it. They tried a remote wipe, but it was blocked. They're attempting a brute-force entry now.
